title: How 'sluggish' initial response may have led to Monday's mess at CJP-led protest
content: Police faced criticism for inaction during the recent 'Sandad Chalo' march.  Intelligence reports provided advance warning of the large demonstrator build-up.  Officers reported a lack of clear orders during the crucial initial hour.  This hesitation allowed crowds to breach key security perimeters and reach Parliament.  Similar administrative hesitations occurred during past protests at Red Fort and India Gate.

title: A fragile fitness system? Hamstring injuries put Team India, CoE under scanner
content: The recurring injuries suffered by Harshit Rana and Nitish Kumar Reddy have not gone down well with the BCCI. Sources said Reddy was being pushed to bowl close to 140kmph to compensate for the injury-prone Hardik Pandya. During Rahul Dravid’s tenure as head coach, the BCCI medical team, the CoE and the team management had charted out year-long roadmaps for individual players around major ICC events. The objective was to get each player to the required fitness level in time for the next ICC tournament or marquee Test series.

title: Larry Ellison & others are buying neighboring houses using 'methods' called 'Landmaxxing’
content: Wealthy individuals are increasingly buying adjacent land to expand their private compounds. This trend, termed 'Landmaxxing', aims to enhance privacy and secure long-term investments. Billionaires like Ken Griffin and Jeff Bezos are actively expanding their estates with significant property acquisitions. Buyers are utilizing these expanded holdings for guest accommodations and recreational facilities. This strategic land acquisition also serves as a hedge against potential future developments and inflation.

title: 'Argentina players shouldn't be suspended': Gavi breaks silence on Paredes clash
content: Spain midfielder Gavi believes Argentina players should not be suspended despite the post-match clashes following Spain’s 1-0 World Cup final win. Although he admitted the scenes were a bad example for children, he said football has an aggressive side and felt any punishment should have come during the match. FIFA has opened an investigation into the incidents.

title: 'We owe students more than outrage': Education minister Pradhan slams Cong protest
content: Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan accused Rahul Gandhi and Congress of exploiting students politically.  He stated they staged a protest outside the Prime Minister's residence, causing public inconvenience.  The minister added that Congress chose spectacle over democratic debate and solutions for students.  He emphasized the government's commitment to discussing NEET and addressing youth concerns.  The government aims to provide answers, reforms, and accountability to students.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: ‘This is gundagardi': Uttarakhand HC raps police for stopping man on way to Delhi protest
content: The Uttarakhand High Court strongly criticized police for detaining activist Prabhat Dhyani.  The court questioned the police's authority to stop citizens exercising their constitutional rights.  Authorities were asked if their role was to protect government image or people's rights.  The court directed the involved officer to be made a party to the case.  Notices were issued to senior police officials for their responses.

title: Florida returned 500,000 oyster shells to Gulf, creating new reef habitat
content: Florida restored parts of the Gulf of Mexico by returning more than 500,000 tonnes of recycled oyster and clam shells to the sea between 2007 and 2024. The shells helped rebuild oyster reefs, improve water quality and support the return of fish, turtles and dolphins to levels not seen in over 25 years.

title: Buffett: Gates knew foundation funds would stop, admits Epstein error, trusts kids with fortune
content: Warren Buffett has confirmed that the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation will no longer receive any future donations from him, ending a philanthropic partnership that lasted more than two decades. The 95-year-old Berkshire Hathaway chairman said the decision was part of a broader review of his estate plans and not a reflection of his personal friendship with Bill Gates. Buffett said Gates was aware of the decision and that the two remain close friends despite recent public scrutiny surrounding Gates.

title: Scientists heard bird sounds beneath floating Solar Farm in Netherlands
content: Floating solar farms in the Netherlands may be supporting local wildlife. Researchers observed bird sounds suggesting possible nesting under the solar panels. Underwater shelters installed beneath the panels attracted hundreds of fish and thousands of aquatic creatures. These structures developed into small ecosystems supporting life higher up the food chain. The findings suggest carefully designed farms could create new habitats for wildlife.

title: Zeeshan denies involvement in Ajay Devgn's Chauhaan
content: Mohd Zeeshan Ayyub clarified he has no connection to Ajay Devgn's 'Chauhaan', explaining he'd dubbed a line "without any reference." His voice was later removed after the teaser sparked backlash from the Chauhan community and Kshatriya Parishad. The film, reportedly inspired by a Jama Masjid incident, releases October 1, 2027.

title: Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day announced: Release date, skins and more
content: Exciting news for Fortnite enthusiasts! A new collaboration featuring Spider-Man has been teased, with a launch date set for July 30, 2026. Players can anticipate a unique Spider-Man skin influenced by the forthcoming movie release. The classic Web Shooters will make a comeback, and a new Sprite could add intriguing web-swinging abilities for a more dynamic gaming experience!

title: Kim Kardashian, Lewis Hamilton romance grows; Kanye West reacts
content: Kim Kardashian seemingly confirmed her relationship with Formula 1 star Lewis Hamilton by sharing a video of them together after their Tokyo trip. Reports claim Hamilton also spent time with her children. Meanwhile, Kanye West is said to have reacted calmly, reportedly respecting Hamilton and trusting him around the kids, a stark contrast to his past public feud with Pete Davidson.

title: Brad Paisley praises Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce's humble wedding
content: Brad Paisley, who attended Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's July 3 wedding at Madison Square Garden with wife Kimberly Williams-Paisley, has described the event as "one of the least pretentious weddings" he has ever attended. Speaking to the Associated Press, Paisley revealed the couple kept the venue a secret until the last moment, calling the invitation process a "cat-and-mouse game." He also praised Swift and Kelce for donating $26 million to charities ahead of the wedding, including his own Nashville-based nonprofit, The Store.

title: Hollywood Hills to Malibu beachfront: Leonardo DiCaprio's multi-million dollar properties
content: Leonardo DiCaprio boasts a remarkable real estate portfolio that spans the United States and Belize. His assets feature a lavish compound in Hollywood Hills, a tranquil private island in Belize, chic apartments in New York City, and a sprawling estate in Palm Springs. Furthermore, DiCaprio has acquired a stunning Spanish Colonial home in Los Feliz, along with a picturesque beachfront property in Malibu and a stylish residence in Beverly Hills.

title: Death note, loans, property: K'taka bizman kills wife, 2 daughters before dying by suicide
content: A Hunsur businessman allegedly killed his wife and two daughters. He then took his own life at their home late at night. Investigators found a death note detailing his assets and loans. The family was discovered by a friend who alerted the police. The motive for the tragic incident remains under investigation.

title: 'Open to both genders': 5,500 apply for Ram Temple Trust CEO post
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust is seeking a new Chief Executive Officer. Over five thousand five hundred applications have been received for the position. A selection committee is currently screening candidates based on experience and qualifications. The appointment of the new CEO is expected around mid-August. The Trust will also fill three vacant trustee positions soon.

title: Argentina players turned their backs during Spain's celebrations; video resurfaces - WATCH
content: Spain defeated Argentina one-nil to win the FIFA World Cup 2026 title.  Post-match scuffles and heated arguments erupted between players on the pitch.  Argentina players turned their backs during Spain's trophy celebration ceremony.  FIFA has now officially opened an investigation into these post-match incidents.  The disciplinary committee will examine potential breaches of the FIFA disciplinary code.

title: Does money buy happiness? What India's spending power reveals about how we live
content: Indian households now spend more on experiences and lifestyle choices.  Average monthly spending has risen across both rural and urban areas.  Food now constitutes a smaller portion of household expenditure.  Easy credit options enable more accessible purchases and lifestyle upgrades.  Despite increased spending, financial security remains a significant concern for many.

title: RRB JE 2026 notification out for 4,098 posts; apply from August 14
content: RRB JE Recruitment 2026: The Railway Recruitment Boards have released CEN No. 04/2026 for recruitment to 4,098 Junior Engineer, Depot Material Superintendent and Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant posts. Online applications are scheduled to open on August 14, 2026, and close on September 13, 2026. Candidates aged 18 to 33 years with the required degree can apply online. The selection process is expected to include two CBTs, document verification and a medical examination.

title: Brent crude tops $92, WTI above $85 as Hormuz tensions threaten global supplies
content: Crude oil prices extended their rally as Middle East tensions escalated.  Brent crude held above ninety dollars a barrel amid supply disruption fears.  Houthi threats affected shipping activity in the Red Sea region.  Military actions continued overnight impacting energy shipping routes.  Global food insecurity risks also increased due to these disruptions.

title: Trump approves Saudi nuclear agreement, opens door to uranium enrichment
content: President Donald Trump approved a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia.  This accord could allow uranium enrichment capabilities for their civilian nuclear program.  US companies are expected to participate in developing Saudi Arabia's nuclear infrastructure.  The agreement follows months of heightened tensions with Iran.  It may pave the way for a uranium enrichment facility in the kingdom.

title: Bruises, bare feet, unwavering resolve: Voices from Jantar Mantar protest
content: Protesters at Jantar Mantar remained determined after police action on Monday. Volunteers provided essential support and medical aid to the injured students. Many young individuals expressed disillusionment with the government's handling of student issues. Older citizens and parents also joined the protest to show solidarity. The gathering vowed to continue until their demands were met.

title: France passes law banning social media for children under 15
content: France has approved a nationwide ban on social media for children under fifteen. This new law also prohibits mobile phone use within high schools across the nation. President Macron wants the ban to begin at the start of the next school year. This measure aims to protect young users from harmful online content and its effects. Several other European countries are also considering similar restrictions on digital platforms.

title: Days after retiring, Ben Stokes marks new chapter with match-winning century
content: Ben Stokes scored an unbeaten 108 for Durham in his first match after retiring from international cricket. He guided his team to a five-wicket victory over Derbyshire in the One-Day Cup. Stokes battled cramp throughout his match-winning innings, staying until the end. Earlier, Derbyshire posted 256/9, with Martin Andersson scoring 112 runs. Stokes recently announced his retirement from all international formats of the game.

title: 'Govt had no interest in debate': Rahul on why Cong protested outside PM's residence
content: Rahul Gandhi addressed students and the Prime Minister concerning the NEET paper leak.  He demanded a proper discussion in Lok Sabha about the examination system.  The Congress party protested outside the Prime Minister's residence over the issue.  Gandhi questioned the collapsing Indian education system and rising exam costs.  He asserted the opposition stands with students for reforms and justice.

title: 'Absolutely zero confidence': Ashwin questions Gambhir & management's faith in Kuldeep
content: Ravichandran Ashwin has questioned Kuldeep Yadav's continued absence from India's team.  The spinner was overlooked for the entire ODI series against England.  Ashwin believes the team management has lost faith in the left-arm wrist spinner.  He suggested Kuldeep should stop waiting for opportunities that may not come.  Kuldeep's lack of game time has sparked significant debate among cricket followers.

title: Health minister JP Nadda, MoS Jitendra Singh meet Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital
content: Union ministers made a notable visit to activist Sonam Wangchuk, currently hospitalized at Medanta in Gurugram. This follows the Delhi High Court's ruling, which facilitated his urgent transfer from Safdarjung Hospital after his wife's appeal contested a previous decision. As Wangchuk enters the 24th day of his hunger strike, protests continue to erupt at Jantar Mantar over the alleged NEET paper leak.

title: 'The Odyssey' crosses Rs 90 crore mark at Indian box office on Day 5
content: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' continues its strong box office run in India. The epic fantasy adventure crossed the Rs 90 crore milestone after a successful opening weekend. Early estimates show the film collected Rs 8.35 crore net on its fifth day. The original English version remains the biggest contributor to the film's collections. 'The Odyssey' is now on its way to hit the Rs 150 crore mark.

title: "Why Go To Protest When I Could Study, Improve My Score": NEET All India Rank 2 To NDTV
content: Panshul Bansal, who secured All India Rank 2 with a 99.9999 percentile in the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination, told NDTV that the cancellation of the original examination initially left him disappointed.

title: High-Speed Chase, Several Dents: Telangana Man Steals Bus, Gets Caught
content: A man stole a Telangana transport bus, injured a motorcyclist, and caused vehicle damage before police arrested him.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Markets Likely To Open Lower As Oil Advances Amid US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude hits $91 as US announces new round of strikes against Iran.

title: "Won't Forget": China Thanks India For Rescuing Crew From Fire-Stricken Tanker
content: Chinese Defence Attache You Jian thanked India's military for rescuing 12 crew members from a burning cargo ship off Kerala last year

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Live Updates: Parliament Set To Resume Today Amid Deadlock Over CJP Protest Crackdown
content: Parliament Monsoon Session Live Updates: The CJP, a movement against paper leaks that started after Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant termed the youngsters "cockroaches", has been sitting at Jantar Mantar since June 20.

title: Iran-US War News Live Updates: US Says It Bombed Iranian Op Centres, Aircraft Hangars, On 11th Night Of Strike
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Delhi Wakes Up To Rain, Weather Office Predicts More Showers Today
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Delhi-NCR experiences rainfall and cooler temperatures as southwest monsoon revives after a lull.

title: Trump Announces Upto 200% Tariffs On Generic Drugs, Move May Impact India
content: India is often called the "pharmacy of the world" as it supplies generic medicines to countries across the globe. In the US, Indian generic medicines make up nearly 40 per cent by volume.

title: Income Tax Return Filing: Why Relying On Deadline Extension Can Be A Costly Mistake
content: A common issue arises when taxpayers log into the IT portal only in the final week of July. This is often when mismatches in financial data surface.

title: JP Nadda, Jitendra Singh Meet Sonam Wangchuk At Gurugram Hospital: Report
content: Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh on Tuesday met activist Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram, sources said.

title: US Military Launches 11th Consecutive Night Of Strikes On Iran
content: US military says it's launched an 11th night of strikes on Iran as they vie for control of choked-off Strait of Hormuz.

title: US Identifies 3rd American Soldier Believed To Be Killed In Iran Attack
content: The US military has confirmed that a third service member is believed to be dead in Friday's attack on a base in Jordan and identified them as Sgt. Angel S. Rampersad, 28, of Ozone Park, New York.

title: Trump Says Imported Generic Drugs To Face 100% Tariff From August 2028
content: US President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday the imposition of steep tariffs on imported generic drugs from August 2028, a move he said was aimed at onshoring production of such pharmaceutical products.

title: 40 People Kidnapped By Nigerian Gang: Report
content: Kidnappings have become a key moneymaking strategy for armed groups in Nigeria, including jihadists in the northeast and non-ideological gangs locally known as bandits who dominate the northwest.

title: 3 Of Hyderabad Family Die In Road Accident In US
content: Three members of a family from Hyderabad died and two others were injured in a road accident in the US state of California, according to information received by their family here on Tuesday.

title: Trump Vows To Help Lebanon Pursue Peace With Israel
content: US President Donald Trump vowed Tuesday to help Lebanon "a lot" as its president, Joseph Aoun, sought Washington's backing for a fragile effort to disarm Hezbollah and forge a path to peace with Israel.

title: Ukraine's Zelensky Sacks Army Chief Amid War
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Tuesday that he was dismissing Oleksandr Syrsky, commander-in-chief of Ukraine's army, and appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place.

title: Pete Hegseth Estimates Iran War Has Cost $37.5 Billion So Far
content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday the Iran war has cost the United States $37.5 billion so far, up from a previous estimate of nearly $29 billion in mid-May.

title: Weather Office Predicts Heavy Rain, Widespread Thundershowers Across UP
content: Rainfall was recorded in different parts of Uttar Pradesh during the 24 hours till Tuesday evening, the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) said, predicting widespread thundershowers across the state over next few days.

title: 21 Killed In 24 Hours As Flood Situation Worsens In Assam
content: The flood situation in Assam further deteriorated on Tuesday as 21 more persons died, while 5.64 lakh people were affected across 16 districts, an official bulletin said.

title: No legal aid to accused, SC orders fresh trial in 30-year-old blast case
content: supreme court sets aside conviction and death sentence of an accused in 1996 samleti bus blast case . the accused, Abdul Hameed, was represented by a lawyer . it concluded that the accused remained undefended in court proceedings .

title: Modi govt picks a Sikh again to head minorities panel
content: Harjit Singh Grewal is the second Sikh to head the body under the Modi govt . the post had been vacant since April 2025, when lalpura's three-year term ended . mr Grewal's appointment gains significance as it comes ahead of 2027 Punjab assembly polls .

title: Doctor, nurse, CEO, caregiver: We tested how AI imagines gender
content: AI models pull bias from decades of text written by people, about people, in a world where women were filed under home and family, and men were filed under business and career. Kanchan Yadav is a writer at The Times of India, where she covers business, politics, international affairs and social issues. Her reporting also focuses on policy, governance and economic developments, with a close lens on how decisions in corridors of power affect everyday lives and community well-being. Read More

title: SC reluctant to take up PIL on paper leaks; asks what and how much can courts do
content: "what can the court do? How much the court can do," a bench of justices says . the bench adjourned the hearing to next month, observing that it was already seized of the case . a petition by advocate said repeated paper leaks leave students in dire straits .

title: India hosts maiden Op Southern Readiness in Kochi, 26 nations join maritime exercise
content: the exercise commenced at the southern Naval Command in Kochi on Monday . it brings together 254 participants from 26 countries . the exercise marks the maiden conduct of Operation Southern Readiness in India . a four-day multinational maritime security training engagement is being conducted under the aegis of the Navy-led Combined Task Force 154 (CTF 154)

title: Indian-origin doctor jailed for secret bathroom recordings, faces sex offender registry
content: an Indian-origin doctor in Ohio's Toledo has been sentenced to six months in jail . he admitted that he secretly recorded women in employee restrooms . the judge ordered Deshmukh to serve three years of probation, register as a sex offender for 15 years and attend sexaholics Anonymous meetings .

title: Sikkim tunnel collapse: 20 bodies recovered from site, state govt order SIT probe
content: at least 20 of the 27 people trapped in a collapsed tunnel have died . two workers have exited the tunnel and search is on for the rest . nine of the dead are from Jalpaiguri district and two from Alipurduar district .

title: 'Dhurandhar lawyers must ensure punishment for paper-leak accused': PM Modi
content: "safety, security and future of the youth is our priority," says parliamentary affairs minister . "Govt will spare no means to ensure stringent punishment to the accused behind NEET paper leak," he quoted modi as saying . gangs involved in paper leaks have been active in many states .

title: Quality sleep key to protecting brain health amid rising screen time, say neurologists
content: Ahead of World Brain Day, observed on July 22, doctors said sleep is not merely a period of rest but a critical biological process during which the brain repairs itself, consolidates memories and clears metabolic waste accumulated during waking hours

title: Protests in New York, San Jose in support of Wangchuk
content: The activists also raised slogans against the Indian government and demanded the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: 46 cadres of banned insurgent group surrender in Manipur
content: The Defence Ministry says that the mass transition is significant, as none of these cadres were part of prior peace agreements

title: U.P. districts conduct physical route inspection, review security for Kanwar Yatra
content: The Uttar Pradesh government has said it has pushed for zero incident, zero accident during the yatra

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Hundreds order food for protesters; Wangchuk shifted to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram
content: The Delhi police on July 21 registered four FIRs against unknown individuals for rioting, assault on public servants, damage to public property and other offences during CJP protest on July 20

title: Parliament breach: Delhi court grants interim bail to accused on humanitarian grounds
content: Sagar Sharma informed the court that his father died of cancer on July 14 and that he was required to perform the last rites and related rituals as the only male member of his family

title: Initiative launched to create Campus Biodiversity Registers across colleges, unversities in India
content: The nationwide exercise to document the flora and fauna within the walls of higher education institutions is anchored by the Nature Conservation Foundation and the National Biodiversity Authority

title: Bihar government recruits 2,446 in JEEVika
content: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary urged the new appointees to work with integrity, honesty, and commitment, so that they can play a vital role in integrating poor people of the society into mainstream

title: Union Education Minister Pradhan says Rahul, Congress exploiting students; CJP's Jantar Mantar protest site draws Opposition leaders | highlights
content: Govt wasted our time, says Dipke on talks between Nadda, CJP team; Sonam Wangchuk shifted to Medanta Hospital

title: Namma Karnataka Sene chief Basawaraj arrested for assault on GBA officials during footpath clearance drive; total arrests rises to 11
content: Police said the investigation is continuing and further action will be taken based on the  mobile phones recording and CCTV evidence collected

title: Rahul changed goalposts to end sit-in, says govt.; BJP terms protest at PM’s residence a ‘symbol of anarchy’
content: Union Minister Jitendra Singh, the government’s interlocutor, says the Congress leader initially agreed to suspend his protest if the paper leak issue was discussed in Parliament, but he later raised the demand for Pradhan’s ouster; Pradhan says Rahul exploiting students as a ‘political tool’

title: Hundreds of people order food online for protestors at Jantar Mantar
content: Food was ordered via food delivery apps from across the country and in some cases, from abroad, with instructions to give it to anyone at the protest site

title: Pellet guns, shock batons used against protesters in Delhi; police deny
content: Shaikh Irshad Mansoori suffered pellet injuries, the first known case of security forces using pellet guns in Delhi; police deny use, say public must “verify facts”

title: 21 dead in a day’s deluge in Assam; total flood toll 31
content: The situation worsened on July 21 after a brief let-up, affecting 5.65 lakh people across 16 districts

title: Jammu and Kashmir grapples weather fury for third day, civilian infrastructure massively damaged
content: The J&K administration decided to keep all educational institutes closed till July 26. Most pilgrimages, including the Amarnath yatra, also remain suspended

title: Congress demands action against former Home Secretary over 1993 firing incident
content: The party has also vowed to strengthen their fight against the BJP and bring a change in the central power in 2029.

title: Outbound portability of medical scheme benefits Rajasthan’s patients
content: Over 5,900 claims amounting to ₹17 crore have been submitted by the patients for speciality treatment in States such as Gujarat, Haryana, Maharashtra and Delhi. These treatments included heart stent procedures, heart bypass surgery, cancer surgery, chemotherapy, dialysis and Caesarean deliveries

title: Farmers stopped at Punjab-Haryana border en route to Delhi rally against India-U.S. trade deal
content: Farmers wanted to peacefully go to Delhi and participate in the rally but the BJP-led Haryana government has stopped them by erecting barricades, Sarwan Singh Pandher, farmer leader and coordinator of the Desh Bachao Morcha, says

title: Former Odisha CM Naveen Patnaik demands resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan
content: Terming the situation “very serious” and “very grave”. he said a number of students were violently attacked and injured

title: Uttar Pradesh cabinet approves free scooty scheme for college girls
content: The scheme will play a significant role in ensuring easier access to higher education for girl students while making them self-reliant and confident, said the State government. Eligible female students will be selected based on merit from the list comprising the top 5% of female graduates

title: Clean energy push is India’s answer to transition challenges: UN climate chief
content: Simon Stiell, Executive Secretary of UN Climate Change, praised India as a “solar superpower”, noting that fossil-fuel-free sources now make up half of its installed power capacity

title: Russian envoy summoned over death of Indian crew
content: The Ministry of External Affairs said that it has conveyed “India’s grave concerns and unequivocal condemnation” over the attack to the Russia envoy

title: Thousands brave rain and regroup at CJP protest site, day after police crackdown
content: From only about 200-300 people early in the morning, crowd swells to thousands by evening; many first-timers say ‘brutal’ police action made them join the protest site

title: NIA court allows special dietary arrangements for U.S. national accused in a terror case
content: Preserving the accused’s dignity requires allowing him access to food he is accustomed to eating, and meals prepared according to his dietary requirements, the defence argues

title: Study to evaluate impact of heat stress on health in Tamil Nadu and Karnataka
content: Participants will receive a digital watch to keep track of body temperature;  1,200 households roped in per site for the study; project to be taken up at Perungudi and Poompuhar in Tamil Nadu, and Sirwar and Chalakarre in Karnataka

title: Gold ornaments, bronze objects stolen from temple
content: The police said that the isolated location of the temple and lack of security measures including CCTV cameras in the temple could have led to the theft

title: BJP condemns Speaker’s observations on DMK MLA arrest
content: BJP State president Nainar Nagenthran said that it was not only a tradition, but also part of rules that the Speaker was to be informed if an MP or an MLA was arrested

title: Around 1.75 tonnes of red sanders stolen from sandalwood depot
content: Forest officials said that based on a complaint by Jothi Lingam who was in charge of the depot, a case was registered by Tirupattur Town police

title: Two persons held for feeding gutka to baby
content: A video of the incident, including that of the child being fed gutka surfaced on social media, inviting public outcry

title: Bengaluru-Mysuru highway toll hiked again after three months
content: According to NHAI toll hike details accessed by The Hindu, the revised rates came into effect at 12 a.m. on July 21

title: Storage in Karnataka’s Cauvery reservoirs stands at 52% of total capacity as CWMA is set to review situation on July 22
content: The Cauvery Water Management Authority, which is the apex body that monitors implementation of Cauvery water release to lower riparian States as per the Supreme Court’s verdict, is meeting in Delhi to review the situation

title: Applications invited for trustees in more temples
content: This time, apart from Aadhar cards and address proof, applicants have been asked to submit a no-objection certificate from the respective temple and a verification certificate from the police

title: Karnataka High Court seeks State’s response on plea seeking CBI probe into ‘illegalities’ in KPSC recruitments
content: The High Court of Karnataka has directed the State government to file its response on a petition seeking a probe by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) into the allegations of illegalities in the Karnataka State Public Service Commission recruitments to posts of Group A and B Gazetted Probationers and allegations against Shivashankar S. Sahukar, suspended chairperson.

title: When India acted tough with Sri Lankan Tamil leaders Anton Balasingham, Chandrahasan in 1985
content: In August 1985, Anton S. Balasingham, who became the best-known international face of the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (LTTE), and S.C. Chandrahasan, founder of Organisation for Eelam Refugees Rehabilitation (OfERR) were served orders to leave India within 48 hours.

title: Kerala eyes major GCC investments with new strategic roadmap
content: Minister Kunhalikutty said the report is a vital step for transforming Kerala into the most preferred GCC destination in the country

title: Toxic gas kills 12 in Sikkim hydropower tunnel
content: According to the NHPC, 25 workers were inside the under-construction tunnel of the Teesta State-VI Hydroelectric Project in Namchi district

title: IIT Roorkee asks students, staff to refrain from political activity without prior approval
content: The advisory comes after the institute says it had noticed on social media platforms posts by some members of campus community expressing support for or criticism of ongoing political movements

title: Unannounced midnight detention of train upsets pilgrims’ travel plans to Guruvayur
content: Passengers says travelling in the new Jan Shatabdi coach seats, which have little or no reclining angle, for four to six hours often results in back pain and a harrowing experience. The earlier trains had reclining seats with armrests.

title: Vazhipadu materials to be procured and issued exclusively by Central Store, TDB tells Kerala HC
content: HC satisfied with TDB decision to discontinue practice of administrative officer acting as supplier for Vazhipadu materials in Sabarimala

title: CFTRI, NABARD join hands to bolster agro-food processing in Kalyana Karnataka
content: The collaboration aims to support the Farmer Training and Common Facility Centres established by NABARD across seven districts, namely Bidar, Yadgir, Kalaburagi, Raichur, Koppal, Ballari and Vijayanagara.

title: Kin of seafarers killed in Hormuz attacks demand UN intervention, compensation, govt. jobs
content: Forward Seamen’s Union of India general secretary Manoj Yadav says Union Government has not raised a strong enough voice against these killings or ensured justice to bereaved families

title: Tamil rapper Arivu detained during bid to protest against NEET near Secretariat in Chennai, CM Vijay meets him
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay invited Arivu — who is best known for his Tamil independent music including the hit song Enjoy Enjaami, Thalapathy Kacheri from ‘Jana Nayagan’ — to his chamber and listened to the singer’s concerns.

title: Tribunal sets aside most of the FEMA violation charges against Lalit Modi, BCCI
content: The Appellate Tribunal under the Smugglers and Foreign Exchange Manipulators (Forfeiture of Property) Act has largely overturned the ED’s conclusions. However, it upheld the ED’s findings on two specific counts

title: Pawar, Kejriwal, other Opposition leaders visit Jantar Mantar after crackdown; back CJP protest
content: Sharad Pawar, accompanied by party MP Supriya Sule, met injured protesters and members of the movement and condemned the police action during Monday's (July 20, 2026) attempted march to Parliament.
